content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To     :    Advice files                                    Date  :  1-20-77


From  :    FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
           Dawn

Subject:   Statements of Economic Interests

     I called Bernice Hudson, City Clerk of Lakeport, on this
date regarding her request for advise.  I told her that
the councilmembers did not have to file for the period
from 12-31-76 to March 9, 1977.  The next filing would
be from 1-1-77 to 12-31-77 and would be due 4-1-78.

CITY OF LAKEPORT,
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

TELEPHONE (707) 263-5682

445 NORTH MAIN STREET
POST OFFICE BOX 115
LAKEPORT, CALIFORNIA 95453

January 17, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

Per copy of your letter dated December 22, 1976, I have the following
inquiry - Do the members of the City Council also have to file Form
721 for the period December 31, 1976, and their anniversary date which
is March 9, 1977.

Your attention to this matter will be greatly appreciated.

Yours very truly,

CITY OF LAKEPORT

[illegible]
BERNICE M. HUDSON
City Clerk

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

December 22, 1976

TO: CITY AND COUNTY CLERKS AND DISTRICT ATTORNEYS

FROM: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND ANALYSIS DIVISION

SUBJECT: STATEMENTS OF ECONOMIC INTERESTS

New legislation chartered in September, 1976 and effective January 1,
1977, requires the Fair Political Practices Commission to establish
the deadline for filing Statements of Economic Interests pursuant to
Government Code Section 87203. The Commission at its December, 1976
meeting adopted a regulation (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18723)
specifying April 1 as the filing deadline for the following officeholders:

Members of County Boards of Supervisors
District Attorneys
Chief Administrative Officers of Counties
Chief Administrative Officers of Cities
Members of City Councils
Mayors
City Managers.

The closing date for the report is December 31, 1976. The report will
cover the period from the closing date of the last statement filed
pursuant to this chapter through December 31, 1976. The statement may
be filed at any time after December 31, 1976 with the city or county
clerk (district attorneys file with their agency) but must be filed no
later than April 1, 1977. The clerk will forward the statement to the
filing officer, the Fair Political Practices Commission within five (5)
days after receipt.

The report must include investments, interests in real property and
income and gifts held or received at any time during the period
covered by the report regardless of whether they are still held at the
time of filing.

NOTE: If a person assumes office between October 1 and December 31
and files a Statement of Economic Interests 10 days prior or 30 days
after assuming office, that person need not file an additional statement
until one year later than the date specified.

RSK:dw

sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing requirements and deadlines for Statements of Economic Interests for city and county officials under the new legislation effective January 1, 1977?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC established April 1, 1977 as the filing deadline for Statements of Economic Interests covering the period through December 31, 1976, for specified officeholders including city council members, mayors, and district attorneys. Statements may be filed anytime after December 31, 1976 but must be received by the clerk no later than April 1, 1977.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both a memorandum regarding a phone call to Lakeport City Clerk and a broader technical assistance letter to clerks and district attorneys. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present, but the content and structure clearly indicate FPPC advice/guidance. Confidence reduced due to document format variation and some text illegibility in the original.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the filing requirements and deadlines for Statements of Economic Interests for city and county officials under the new legislation effective January 1, 1977?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC established April 1, 1977 as the filing deadline for Statements of Economic Interests covering the period through December 31, 1976, for specified officeholders including city council members, mayors, and district attorneys. Statements may be filed anytime after December 31, 1976 but must be received by the clerk no later than April 1, 1977.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice document establishes the filing deadline and requirements for Statements of Economic Interests under new legislation effective January 1, 1977. It specifies April 1, 1977 as the deadline for various officeholders and clarifies the reporting period and procedures.
